title: Evangelion on Religion
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion on Religion             Quote Post by kei_evageek_phl » Thu Jun 30, 2011 2:14 am I was able to watch the "Death and Rebirth" so that I will learn something about Evangelion.... But, one thing that kind of stuck in my mind.... Some of terms that were used in NGE were Religion related, esp. Catholicism Terms........ Does the Creators of the Anime really did that on purpose? Which reminds me of one thing, "Neon Genesis Evangelion" is compose of Religion-related terms: "Neon Genesis"(or "New Genesis") which came from either the first book of the old testament, the Book of Genesis, esp. the Story of New Creation. And, "Evangelion" which means the 4 scriptures of the 4 evangelists. Hope you will answer/reply my inquiries.. Because, even if I'm an EVA geek, I didn't quite watch the series.. Because it didn't show here in my country..... Thanks....:)

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jun 30, 2011 2:18 am You didn't notice the religious themes until you saw Death & Rebirth? Has that been the only Eva-related media you've seen? There's a lot of this sort of thing littered throughout the entire show, and it's not limited to only Catholic themes. If you're trying to find a reason behind it, it mostly just boils down to "it looks cool."

[]:             Quote Post by kei_evageek_phl » Thu Jun 30, 2011 2:22 am I also watch End of Evangelion.......... I'm kind of confused and questioned so that's why I posted this... But, thanks for the comment....... I kind of learned by it.....

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jun 30, 2011 2:30 am Oh man. If you have any interest in Evangelion, I would definitely suggest getting your hands on the show and watching through all 26 episodes of that, then rewatching End of Evangelion. It should make a whole lot more sense.

[]:             Quote Post by kei_evageek_phl » Thu Jun 30, 2011 2:31 am thanks.........

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Jun 30, 2011 2:36 am yeah first of all watch tv series, then end of evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jun 30, 2011 2:45 am And don't forget the FAQ and Wiki too http://forum.evageeks.org/faq.php http://wiki.evageeks.org/

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Jun 30, 2011 4:23 am Finally, beware of any statements made by Mr. Snausages*. *It's another name for a self-proclaimed fan who insists that there is no religious connection with Eva, although he's struggling to set up a cult of his own. Nuff said.

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Thu Jun 30, 2011 7:06 am You can find every episode on the internets. Yar har fiddle-dee-dee

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Jun 30, 2011 7:39 am But we cant tell you where. But know that Google is your friend.

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Thu Jun 30, 2011 8:39 am try watching the entire series while being sober, then watch it again while drunk(Japanese alcohol preferred), and see the difference urself (the only difference i felt as that the beer tasted 100% better when watching Misato drink it too)

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Jun 30, 2011 12:40 pm If you really want to jump down the rabbit hole, try doing a little research on Kabbalah, Gnosticism, and other Jewish & Christian apocrypha on the periphery. I think you'll start to see some connections. And remember: don't let anyone tell you what to believe about Evangelion .

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jun 30, 2011 12:45 pm gatotsu911 wrote: And remember: don't let anyone tell you what to believe about Evangelion .

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Jun 30, 2011 1:01 pm symbv wrote: Including the official sources, so you can revive the theory of "Misato killed Kaji" again

[]: Re: Evangelion on Religion             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Thu Jun 30, 2011 3:59 pm kei_evageek_phl wrote: I was able to watch the "Death and Rebirth" so that I will learn something about Evangelion.... But, one thing that kind of stuck in my mind.... Some of terms that were used in NGE were Religion related, esp. Catholicism Terms........ Does the Creators of the Anime really did that on purpose? Which reminds me of one thing, "Neon Genesis Evangelion" is compose of Religion-related terms: "Neon Genesis"(or "New Genesis") which came from either the first book of the old testament, the Book of Genesis, esp. the Story of New Creation. And, "Evangelion" which means the 4 scriptures of the 4 evangelists. Hope you will answer/reply my inquiries.. Because, even if I'm an EVA geek, I didn't quite watch the series.. Because it didn't show here in my country..... Thanks....:)

[]: Re: Evangelion on Religion             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Jun 30, 2011 4:14 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: We Catholics left and resounding mark on their culture, and a positive one at that.

[]: Re: Evangelion on Religion             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Jun 30, 2011 4:36 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Citation needed

[]: Re: Evangelion on Religion             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Jun 30, 2011 4:58 pm Allemann wrote: Others don't need to make up for your lack of knowledge.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Jun 30, 2011 5:50 pm Three of Japanese prime minsteres were Catholics; Shusaku Endo and Ayako Sono, major literary writers in post-war Japan, were also Catholics; there's the Marian apparition, The Lady of Akita; the inventor of the Suzuki method of music education is also a Catholic; and then there's also Koichi Mashimo, the founder of the BeeTrain animation studio.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Thu Jun 30, 2011 7:16 pm Guys try not to derail this thread. Anyways as for the OP, make what you will of the various Christian and Jewish references littered throughout the series. Personally, I think all the symbolism is there for mostly aesthetic value.
